heroHeadline: Artemis II Mission Complete — Crew Splashes Down After Historic Lunar Flyby
heroSubtitle: Flight Day 10 (Apr 10): Orion splashes down off San Diego at ~8:07 p.m. EDT, completing humanity's first crewed lunar flyby in over 50 years. The crew — Wiseman, Glover, Koch, and Hansen — set the human deep-space distance record (252,706 mi) and validated the Orion capsule for the crewed lunar landing now targeted for Artemis IV in 2028.
footerNote: Data sourced from NASA.gov, OIG Reports, SpaceNews, Ars Technica, Congressional appropriations. Tier 1: NASA/ESA/Official. Tier 2: SpaceNews/Ars Technica/Reuters. Tier 3: Planetary Society/AIAA.
